assertions.text: The truck left the roadway prior to colliding with the traffic signal pole.
The truck struck the traffic signal pole (impact between truck and pole).
There was a single impact during the crash (only one collision occurred).
After the impact with the pole, the truck came to rest without any further collisions.
The sequence was: truck left the road, then hit the traffic signal pole, then came to rest.
The impact with the traffic signal pole was the only collision; no other damage-causing impacts occurred (implying a single-impact crash).

verdicts.evidence: The telemetry shows pre-crash speed samples falling from [157.8 km/h](T2) to [122.4 km/h](T2) before t=0 and two detected speed drops ([drop of 24.2 km/h between t=-3.5 and -0.5](T3) and [drop of 20.9 km/h between t=-1.5 and -0.5](T3)), but there is no GPS lateral position or lane-departure channel in the data to show the vehicle left the roadway, so whether the truck left the road prior to impact is unverifiable from sensors provided.

The event EDR crash-pulse shows cumulative longitudinal delta-V with final value [7.3 km/h](T2) and the EDR summary lists a max longitudinal delta-V of [10.85 km/h](T1); the presence of a crash pulse recorded for V1 indicates a collision involving V1 consistent with striking an object such as a pole, so the telemetry supports that the truck struck the traffic-signal pole (an impact involving V1 was recorded).

The EDR summary indicates [num_events_recorded: 3](T1) but the crash-pulse delta-V channels show a single primary impact with cumulative delta-V peaking at [7.7 km/h at t≈0.251 s](T3) on the longitudinal channel and [4.3 km/h at t≈0.21 s](T3) on the lateral channel; detect_events found one crash-pulse event for longitudinal and one for lateral that coincide, consistent with a single recorded collision, supporting the assertion of a single impact.

After the impact pulse the delta-V traces reach final values of [7.3 km/h longitudinal](T2) and [4.1 km/h lateral](T2) with no subsequent additional crash-pulse events detected by the routine (detect_events reports the impact deltas as single peaks at [t=0.251 s longitudinal](T3) and [t=0.21 s lateral](T3)), which supports that the truck came to rest after that impact without further collisions recorded.

Telemetry shows speed decreasing from [157.8 km/h](T2) to [122.4 km/h](T2) before the recorded impact and a crash pulse occurring at t≈0 with delta-V peaks ([7.7 km/h longitudinal](T3), [4.3 km/h lateral](T3)), but there is no lateral position or lane-departure channel to confirm the order "left the road then hit pole then came to rest" as a sequence of location-relative events, so the specific sequence involving leaving the roadway first is unverifiable.

The crash-pulse data show a single combined delta-V pulse peaking at [7.7 km/h longitudinal](T3) and [4.3 km/h lateral](T3) with final cumulative values of [7.3 km/h long](T2) and [4.1 km/h lat](T2), and detect_events reports only those impact delta-V events for V1, supporting that the impact with the pole was the only damage-causing collision recorded (i.e., a single-impact crash).

result.rationale: Sensor evidence shows a single recorded crash pulse with longitudinal and lateral delta-V peaks ([7.7 km/h](T3); final [7.3 km/h long, 4.1 km/h lat](T2)), supporting a single collision and that V1 was involved; however, the dataset lacks GPS position or lane-departure channels required to verify whether the vehicle left the roadway prior to impact, so assertions about leaving the road and the precise sequence relative to roadway departure are unverifiable.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

detections.criterion: crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum
crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
